threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus drivers will help you with directions. Just keep an eye out; they're not your tour guides, mate.
Caught the 59 last night; didn’t expect a guy to serenade the whole bus with 90s R&B. Some joined in. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? When I heard a street performer covering 90s Eurodance while kids joyfully danced in the rain at Hermannplatz.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bear-shaped fountain surrounded by graffiti murals, where kids splash and skaters chill. It's a quirky little gem.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows to avoid the Civic Center BART during rush hour—too many 'hands up, don’t touch' moments with the overheated brakes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 earlier and a guy was serenading the bus with a ukulele. Only in SF would a lullaby clash with a loud horn outside.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every street in Gràcia is a photo op, but the real charm lies in the small, hidden plazas where locals gather over cheap beers.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by the way they still ask for a 'tapa' and expect a plate instead of a tiny portion. We all did it once.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's a great idea to bring your coffee into the pub—not knowing that's a surefire way to get side-eyed.
Caught the 36 last night; a guy serenaded the driver with 'Wonderwall' while the rest of us just rolled our eyes. Only in Peckham!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw my neighbor grabbing their morning coffee and chatting with the Turkish baker about the best local spots. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club where they serve vegan currywurst at 4 AM while you dance to techno. It's a wild blend that only Berlin nails.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name all the taquerias that have survived since the '90s and know the exact order of their favorite burritos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo hopping off the Muni next to a food truck – just another Tuesday night in the TL, where attire means nothing.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just Gaudí's playground, but the real treasures are the corner bakeries where locals get their morning ensaïmades.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to order tapas without a hint of 'por favor' in their accent, clearly lost in translation.
